goals.description: Confront teammate_A face-to-face to scream about the betrayal and force a direct renegotiation of our team's reputation.
goals.rationale: I can't just send a text anymore. The prof already knows, and sending more words feels weak after screaming into the void. I need to make my anger visible, face-to-face, so they know exactly who they messed with. If I don't confront them loudly right now, I'll feel like I'm still hiding behind a screen while they steal credit. My affiliation drive demands we clear the air physically; if they lie again in front of me, I'll drop their name publicly. It hurts my head to be this angry, but staying silent makes me look guilty. I have to scream it out NOW.

goals.description: Immediately retreat to cohort_3 for support and venting once the confrontation is done.
goals.rationale: I just screamed at one person, now I need to scream at people who actually care about me. My short planning horizon says: deal with the immediate emotional spike, then move on. I'm too exhausted to think strategically right now; I just need to get back to my tribe. If I let myself isolate here, the stress will eat my sleep and ruin everything. Getting through this cycle with my friends is non-negotiable tonight.

agent.last_reasoning: I just sent the prof a threat to scream. My affiliation drive demands I actually show up and make them feel it face-to-face, not waste energy on filler work. If I don't confront them NOW, my anger will rot and I'll look weak later. Waiting is the only way to get to that physical confrontation.

goals.description: Collapse onto the couch with cohort_3 for dinner tonight.
